channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: In the Ukrainian city of Sumy, life goes on despite the constant threat of attack
item.description: The humdrum of daily life in Sumy belies the constant threat of death its people have lived with since Russia invaded Ukraine three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Samya Kullab And Yehor Konovalov, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China freezes Boeing jet sales in trade tit-for-tat with Trump: Report
item.description: China has ordered its airlines to ditch jet deliveries from Boeing following days of back-and-forth with President Trump over hikes in tariffs. The president’s 145 percent levy on Chinese goods drove the decision, according to Bloomberg, which first reported the stalemate on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: France expels 12 Algerian officials in tit-for-tat move amid diplomatic tensions
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France said Tuesday it was expelling 12 Algerian diplomatic officials a day after Algeria announced the expulsion of the same number of French officials in escalating tensions between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: NATO chief reaffirms support for Ukraine during a visit to the port city of Odesa
item.description: ODESA, Ukraine (AP) — NATO’s support for Ukraine remains “unwavering,” the alliance’s secretary-general said Tuesday, emphasizing that more than 20 billion euros — over $22 billion — in security assistance has already been pledged by NATO allies in the first three months of the year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia sentences 4 journalists tied to Putin rival Navalny to nearly 6 years in prison
item.description: Four journalists were convicted of extremism on Tuesday by a Russian court and sentenced to 5 and a half years in prison for their ties to the country’s deceased opposition leader Alexei Navalny.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump envoy backtracks on remarks about limiting Iran's nuclear program
item.description: Steve Witkoff, President Trump’s point person on talks with Iran, clarified earlier remarks on Tuesday saying the White House could tolerate Iran having a civilian nuclear program, saying it wants Tehran to eliminate its nuclear enrichment and weapons program.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Israeli airstrike hits hospital entrance in Gaza, killing medic and wounding 9 other people
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit the northern gate of a field hospital in the Gaza Strip on Tuesday, killing a medic and wounding nine other people, a hospital spokesman said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Another US aircraft carrier in Mideast waters ahead of second round of Iran-US nuclear talks
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — A second U.S. aircraft carrier is operating in Mideast waters ahead of the next round of talks between Iran and the United States over Tehran's rapidly advancing nuclear program, satellite photos analyzed Tuesday by The Associated Press showed.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chinese Embassy accuses Bessent of 'maliciously slandering' Beijing
item.description: The Chinese Embassy in Argentina has criticized U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent for “maliciously slandering” the country as it continues to fund development assistance for African and Latin American countries. “We advise the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Witkoff says US 'finally' got answer on Putin's demands for peace
item.description: U.S. special envoy Steve Witkoff said Monday that he “finally” got an answer to Russian President Vladimir Putin’s demands for peace during their meeting last week in Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Tens of thousands of Jews gather for traditional blessing in Jerusalem
item.description: Tens of thousands of people have gathered at the holiest Jewish prayer site in Jerusalem for the traditional priestly blessing prayer.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: People detained in Myanmar after release from scam compounds attempt an escape
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — They walked out of the compound in Myanmar not knowing where they would go. Though they were aiming for the river that separated them from Thailand and freedom, they didn't know if they would make it across.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hungary passes constitutional amendment to ban LGBTQ+ public events, seen as a major blow to rights
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hungary’s parliament on Monday passed an amendment to the constitution that allows the government to ban public events by LGBTQ+ communities, a decision that legal scholars and critics call another step toward authoritarianism by the populist government.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US flies long-range bomber for drill with South Korea, as the North marks a key state anniversary
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The United States flew long-range B-1B bombers in a show of force against North Korea on Tuesday, days after the powerful sister of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un vowed to resist a U.S.-led push to eliminate the North’s nuclear program. North Korea often reacts to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN: Civilian death toll in Sudan's Darfur attacks tops 300 in 2 days as war nears 2-year mark
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — More than 300 civilians were killed in two days of intense fighting in Sudan’s conflict-wracked Darfur region, the U.N. humanitarian agency reported Monday, as the African country's brutal civil war nears the two-year mark.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An international conference rallies aid for Sudan after 2 years of civil war, but peace is elusive
item.description: Britain and the European Union have pledged hundreds of millions of dollars to ease suffering in Sudan.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New York City's mayor visits the Dominican Republic after the deadly nightclub roof collapse
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams arrived in the Dominican Republic on Monday to pay his respects to the scores of people who died last week when the roof of a popular nightclub collapsed in the country's capital, Santo Domingo.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin sees Trump as 'an easy mark': John Bolton
item.description: During Monday's meeting with the press, Trump said of Ukraine: "When you start a war, you've gotta know that you can win the war.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Britain's 'Stop Brexit Man' acquitted by judge over his musical protests outside Parliament
item.description: LONDON (AP) — An anti- Brexit activist who has spent years mounting a one-man protest outside Britain’s Parliament won a court victory on Monday against a police attempt to pull the plug on his musical activities.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poland says Moscow is 'mocking' Trump with deadly Ukraine strike
item.description: Poland is urging the U.S. to respond to Russian President Vladimir Putin's "mocking" response to President Donald Trump's attempts to secure peace deal by striking civilians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump hosts El Salvador's Nayib Bukele amid deportation controversy
item.description: Since March, El Salvador has accepted more than 200 Venezuelan immigrants accused by the U.S. of gang activity and violent crimes.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Home to centuries of heritage, Sudan's biggest museum is looted and wrecked by a 2-year war
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Inside Sudan's biggest museum, the exhibition halls once filled with statues and relics from centuries of ancient civilizations are trashed, littered with debris. The display cases stand empty and shattered. A mummy lies exposed in an open storage box.
item.dc:creator: FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ecuador re-elects President Daniel Noboa amid nationwide unease over crime
item.description: Conservative Ecuadorian President Daniel Noboa, a wealthy anti-crime crusader, was re-elected on Sunday, defeating left-wing lawyer Luisa González.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 50 people killed in east Congo as government and Rwanda-backed rebels trade blame
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — At least 50 people were killed in weekend attacks in Congo’s conflict-battered east, authorities said Saturday. The government traded blame with Rwanda-backed rebels over who was responsible for the violence that quickly escalated the conflict in the region.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and JEAN-YVES KAMALE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel intensifies strikes across Gaza on Palm Sunday and hits a hospital in the north
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza (AP) — A wave of Israeli strikes across Gaza on Sunday hit a hospital, a municipal building, a home and a vehicle, killing at least 21 people, including children, as Israel vowed to expand its security presence in the small coastal strip.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump: Russia-Ukraine talks at 'put up or shut up' point
item.description: Trump's comments came after a top envoy traveled to Russia to meet directly with President Vladimir Putin on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Tragic NYC helicopter crash hits home in Barcelona, where victims were well known
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — As they sat inside a helicopter shortly before a flight over the towering skyscrapers of New York City, Agustin Escobar flashed a thumbs-up while his wife and children beamed big smiles.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump admin ordered to return alleged MS-13 member mistakenly deported
item.description: The justices did not say exactly what Kilmar Abrego Garcia's release would entail or how he would return to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Greenpeace UK leader arrested for targeting American embassy with ' blood-red dye’: report
item.description: Greenpeace’s U.K. leader and five other activists reportedly were arrested after tossing hundreds of liters of “blood-red dye" into a pond at the U.S. embassy in London.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Officials scramble to ID victims of Dominican nightclub roof collapse
item.description: Crews at the scene were still looking for victims and potential survivors, although no one has been found alive since Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Countries will follow Trump imposing tariffs on China: Gordon Chang
item.description: Chang, author of the new book "Plan Red: China's Project to Destroy America," said he expects to see tariffs rise on Chinese goods worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine says more than 150 Chinese mercenaries are fighting for Russia in Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Wednesday expanded on its claim that significant numbers of Chinese nationals are fighting for Russia’s invading army, saying it had gathered detailed intelligence on more than 150 mercenaries Moscow allegedly recruited through social media.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, WGN 9
